rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on core constructs—subgoal success/completion and persistence toward long-term goals—and the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior subgoal-success research while testing financial contexts. The cited paper provides a prominent field demonstration in debt management that subgoal completion predicts persistence outcomes, making it a plausible theoretical/empirical foundation rather than an incidental mention, even though only a DOI-level match is available.
